title: agropyron cristatum var. pectinatum
intro: Agropyron cristatum var. pectinatum, also known as Pectin Grass or Pectin Brome, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, where it has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Pectin Grass include its ability to soothe stomach ulcers, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or infusion, or its roots are used to make tinctures and decoctions for medicinal purposes.
